key_insight: An end-to-end machine learning pipeline can detect moles in wide-angle skin photos, segment lesions, and extract ABCD(E)-style features to support automated melanoma screening.
clinical_relevance: The system is designed to help triage suspicious lesions and standardize mole assessment, especially when expert review time is limited.
method: Adaptive thresholding + blob analysis to detect candidate moles
EDSR super-resolution and brightness-based filtering to reduce false positives
BAM lesion segmentation with EfficientNet-B7 + GradCAM fusion, then K-means color scoring for ABCD feature output
evidence_signal: In the abstract, BAM reached 90.45% accuracy on ISIC2017, EfficientNet-B7 achieved melanoma AUC 0.99, and mole detection on 87 wide-angle images achieved F1 = 86%.
watch_item: Performance was reported on specific datasets and a small set of wide-angle images, so broader real-world validation and expert concordance are still needed.
paper_title: Automated Melanoma Screening: A Machine Learning Pipeline for Mole Detection, Boundary Segmentation, and ABCD(E) Feature Extraction
